---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 65d2bea679a3fb020f99c05be4ae621c7f8b8575
findings:
  - { id: judged-f1, kind: judged, text: "Restatement of the known, ledger-dispositioned deviation (accepted-deviation per ADJ-40, reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47), carried so this sweep's record stays complete at the new HEAD: ac-3's letter says 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:81-83) returns before writing anything — no \u003csection data-testid=\"home-glance\"\u003e, no bucket headings, no zero counts — so a failed-index render carries zero glance structure. The build resolves ac-3's universal quantifier against co-2's failure clause ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the whole section, a reading ratified on the ledger but still not declared in the spec text itself, and the failure render is pinned only by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, never by a Playwright drive of GET / under a failing index (co-1's 'every behavioral path is Playwright-proven' letter). (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Restatement of the standing index-failure class per ADJ-40 (reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47): co-2's failure clause bounds ac-3's universal quantifier, so full-section omission on a failed index is the contract's own design, pinned by TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing per ADJ-40's unit-pinnable-negative-path reasoning. Re-recorded on the regenerated text per ADJ-59." }
  - { id: judged-f2, kind: judged, text: "The ADJ-35 residual divergence path — a glance-admitted active-zone entry whose spec.md is absent from the serving checkout, where dc-3 and ac-1's own text promise the board link (and the nested matrix/verdict) is 'honestly withheld, exactly as the exhaustive section degrades' and the title falls back to the ref — is pinned only by a Go unit test (internal/workbench/glance_test.go ~280-287), never by a Playwright drive of GET / over a fixture store whose refs and working tree diverge. co-1's letter requires every behavioral path Playwright-proven, and the existing accepted-deviation chain (ADJ-40/ADJ-44/ADJ-47) covers only the index-failure negative render, not this spec-described degradation path; no ledger disposition names it. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Accepted-deviation per Controller adjudication ADJ-64 (2026-07-17), test-honesty angle: the ADJ-35 withheld-link degradation (serving-checkout-absent spec.md) is Go-unit-proven not Playwright-proven, and in-browser fixturing of a refs/working-tree divergence is disproportionate per ADJ-42, so this rides to the Phase-5 test-honesty queue. Re-recorded on the regenerated text per ADJ-59." }
  - { id: judged-f3, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's evidence letter says 'every pre-existing data-testid this story does not itself introduce is asserted present, visible, and carrying the content it carries today' by Playwright 're-assert[ing] every pre-existing surface unchanged on the same render that also proves AC-1'. The implementation's ac-2 proof (e2e/tests/43-home-status-glance.spec.ts, second test) is a separate page.goto from the ac-1 test — a different render — and re-asserts a representative sample (the four dir-group testids, the glance-shared entries, the archived and disclosed entries, and bare section-shell visibility for .home-kinds/.home-services/.home-boards/.store-root/.home-disclosures) rather than every pre-existing testid with its content; the exhaustive section's chips, per-entry links, and section contents are covered only in aggregate by the pre-existing 37-directory-home suite over the same store. Deterministic rendering over the identical fixture store makes this materially equivalent, but it narrows ac-2's stated same-render, every-testid evidence shape without a declared disposition. (confidence 0.25)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Accepted-deviation per ADJ-64's closing clause: genuinely-new, confidence 0.25 (sub-0.50) and non-behavioral (a test-evidence-shape narrowing; ac-2's every-testid same-render shape is proven by a representative sample plus the pre-existing 37-directory-home suite over the identical deterministic fixture store, materially equivalent), so no behavioral defect is alleged. Reported verbatim for merge review; filed for the Phase-5 test-honesty angle." }
digest: sha256:2edab55c17d189def1c91ac59bd6873c0e4602c8b6ef9b689ac65472b0043bcf
integrity: sha256:7c73ace6571eb3edc9d553308a7f97e27675978df89b245cf67439f498f2d56d
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f1\",\"text\":\"Restatement of the known, ledger-dispositioned deviation (accepted-deviation per ADJ-40, reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47), carried so this sweep's record stays complete at the new HEAD: ac-3's letter says 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:81-83) returns before writing anything — no \u003csection data-testid=\\\"home-glance\\\"\u003e, no bucket headings, no zero counts — so a failed-index render carries zero glance structure. The build resolves ac-3's universal quantifier against co-2's failure clause ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the whole section, a reading ratified on the ledger but still not declared in the spec text itself, and the failure render is pinned only by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, never by a Playwright drive of GET / under a failing index (co-1's 'every behavioral path is Playwright-proven' letter).\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"f2\",\"text\":\"The ADJ-35 residual divergence path — a glance-admitted active-zone entry whose spec.md is absent from the serving checkout, where dc-3 and ac-1's own text promise the board link (and the nested matrix/verdict) is 'honestly withheld, exactly as the exhaustive section degrades' and the title falls back to the ref — is pinned only by a Go unit test (internal/workbench/glance_test.go ~280-287), never by a Playwright drive of GET / over a fixture store whose refs and working tree diverge. co-1's letter requires every behavioral path Playwright-proven, and the existing accepted-deviation chain (ADJ-40/ADJ-44/ADJ-47) covers only the index-failure negative render, not this spec-described degradation path; no ledger disposition names it.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"f3\",\"text\":\"ac-2's evidence letter says 'every pre-existing data-testid this story does not itself introduce is asserted present, visible, and carrying the content it carries today' by Playwright 're-assert[ing] every pre-existing surface unchanged on the same render that also proves AC-1'. The implementation's ac-2 proof (e2e/tests/43-home-status-glance.spec.ts, second test) is a separate page.goto from the ac-1 test — a different render — and re-asserts a representative sample (the four dir-group testids, the glance-shared entries, the archived and disclosed entries, and bare section-shell visibility for .home-kinds/.home-services/.home-boards/.store-root/.home-disclosures) rather than every pre-existing testid with its content; the exhaustive section's chips, per-entry links, and section contents are covered only in aggregate by the pre-existing 37-directory-home suite over the same store. Deterministic rendering over the identical fixture store makes this materially equivalent, but it narrows ac-2's stated same-render, every-testid evidence shape without a declared disposition.\",\"confidence\":0.25}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-17, commit: 65d2bea679a3fb020f99c05be4ae621c7f8b8575 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/home-status-glance@65d2bea679a3fb020f99c05be4ae621c7f8b8575, spec/home-status-glance@28024ab6c2631d07f449df8178b6c26b70c14787], digest: sha256:2edab55c17d189def1c91ac59bd6873c0e4602c8b6ef9b689ac65472b0043bcf, integrity: sha256:7c73ace6571eb3edc9d553308a7f97e27675978df89b245cf67439f498f2d56d }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-f1** [accepted-deviation]: Restatement of the known, ledger-dispositioned deviation (accepted-deviation per ADJ-40, reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47), carried so this sweep's record stays complete at the new HEAD: ac-3's letter says 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:81-83) returns before writing anything — no <section data-testid="home-glance">, no bucket headings, no zero counts — so a failed-index render carries zero glance structure. The build resolves ac-3's universal quantifier against co-2's failure clause ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the whole section, a reading ratified on the ledger but still not declared in the spec text itself, and the failure render is pinned only by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, never by a Playwright drive of GET / under a failing index (co-1's 'every behavioral path is Playwright-proven' letter). (confidence 0.30) — Restatement of the standing index-failure class per ADJ-40 (reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47): co-2's failure clause bounds ac-3's universal quantifier, so full-section omission on a failed index is the contract's own design, pinned by TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing per ADJ-40's unit-pinnable-negative-path reasoning. Re-recorded on the regenerated text per ADJ-59.
- **judged-f2** [accepted-deviation]: The ADJ-35 residual divergence path — a glance-admitted active-zone entry whose spec.md is absent from the serving checkout, where dc-3 and ac-1's own text promise the board link (and the nested matrix/verdict) is 'honestly withheld, exactly as the exhaustive section degrades' and the title falls back to the ref — is pinned only by a Go unit test (internal/workbench/glance_test.go ~280-287), never by a Playwright drive of GET / over a fixture store whose refs and working tree diverge. co-1's letter requires every behavioral path Playwright-proven, and the existing accepted-deviation chain (ADJ-40/ADJ-44/ADJ-47) covers only the index-failure negative render, not this spec-described degradation path; no ledger disposition names it. (confidence 0.35) — Accepted-deviation per Controller adjudication ADJ-64 (2026-07-17), test-honesty angle: the ADJ-35 withheld-link degradation (serving-checkout-absent spec.md) is Go-unit-proven not Playwright-proven, and in-browser fixturing of a refs/working-tree divergence is disproportionate per ADJ-42, so this rides to the Phase-5 test-honesty queue. Re-recorded on the regenerated text per ADJ-59.
- **judged-f3** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2's evidence letter says 'every pre-existing data-testid this story does not itself introduce is asserted present, visible, and carrying the content it carries today' by Playwright 're-assert[ing] every pre-existing surface unchanged on the same render that also proves AC-1'. The implementation's ac-2 proof (e2e/tests/43-home-status-glance.spec.ts, second test) is a separate page.goto from the ac-1 test — a different render — and re-asserts a representative sample (the four dir-group testids, the glance-shared entries, the archived and disclosed entries, and bare section-shell visibility for .home-kinds/.home-services/.home-boards/.store-root/.home-disclosures) rather than every pre-existing testid with its content; the exhaustive section's chips, per-entry links, and section contents are covered only in aggregate by the pre-existing 37-directory-home suite over the same store. Deterministic rendering over the identical fixture store makes this materially equivalent, but it narrows ac-2's stated same-render, every-testid evidence shape without a declared disposition. (confidence 0.25) — Accepted-deviation per ADJ-64's closing clause: genuinely-new, confidence 0.25 (sub-0.50) and non-behavioral (a test-evidence-shape narrowing; ac-2's every-testid same-render shape is proven by a representative sample plus the pre-existing 37-directory-home suite over the identical deterministic fixture store, materially equivalent), so no behavioral defect is alleged. Reported verbatim for merge review; filed for the Phase-5 test-honesty angle.
